shortDescription: A practical course for building a small language model: dataset, tokenizer, baseline, neural LM, evaluation, and generation.
fullDescription:
This course guides students through the full workflow of building a small language model: preparing a text dataset, creating a character tokenizer, training a bigram baseline, training a neural character LM, generating text, evaluating models, and building a simple deployment demo. Each lab adds one new idea and saves artifacts that can be checked automatically.

Image attribution: Cluster image adapted from Circumference C25 by Gareth Halfacree. Licensed under CC BY-SA 2.0. Source: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Circumference_C25_(40321335160).png. License: https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/2.0/. Cropped and stylized for use on Ondroid.
requirements: Basic Python: functions, lists, dictionaries, reading and writing files, and JSON. Basic command-line familiarity is helpful but not required.
